title: jacquemontia hewittacea
intro: Jacquemontia hewittacea, also known as Hewitt's morning glory, is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family (Convolvulaceae). It is native to Southeast Asia and has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Jacquemontia hewittacea include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or poultices to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
